stem: Newton's method for \( g(x)=x^{2}-[[N]] \) uses \( x_{k+1}=x_k-\frac{g(x_k)}{g'(x_k)} \). Starting at \( x_0=[[x0]] \), find \( x_{[[n]]} \).
solution: Applying the Newton iteration \( [[n]] \) time(s) from \( [[x0]] \) gives \( [[answer]] \).

stem: To approximate the cube root of \( [[N]] \) via Newton's method, apply \( x_{k+1}=x_k-\dfrac{x_k^{3}-[[N]]}{3x_k^{2}} \) starting from \( x_0=[[x0]] \). Compute \( x_{[[n]]} \).
solution: Here \( g'(x)=3x^{2} \); applying the Newton iteration \( [[n]] \) time(s) from \( [[x0]] \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This misweights the average of \( x_0 \) and \( N/x_0^2 \) (uses the square-root update's weighting).
This forgets to divide the correction term by \( g'(x_0)=3x_0^{2} \).
This reports \( N/x_0^{2} \) alone rather than the Newton update.
This leaves \( x \) unchanged from \( x_0 \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Newton's method estimates a root of \( f(x)=x^{3}+([[b]])x^{2}+([[c]])x+([[d]]) \) using \( x_{k+1}=x_k-\frac{f(x_k)}{f'(x_k)} \). Starting at \( x_0=[[x0]] \), find \( x_{[[n]]} \).
solution: With \( f'(x)=3x^{2}+2([[b]])x+([[c]]) \), applying the Newton iteration \( [[n]] \) time(s) from \( x_0=[[x0]] \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide the correction \( f(x_0) \) by \( f'(x_0) \).
This has the wrong sign on the correction term.
This leaves \( x \) unchanged from \( x_0 \).
This flips the sign of the starting point instead of applying Newton's update.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Newton's method for finding \( \dfrac{1}{[[N]]} \) uses \( g(x)=\dfrac{1}{x}-[[N]] \), which (avoiding division in the update itself) simplifies to the iteration \( x_{k+1}=x_k(2-[[N]]x_k) \). Starting at \( x_0=[[x0]] \), find \( x_{[[n]]} \).
solution: Applying \( x_{k+1}=x_k(2-[[N]]x_k) \) \( [[n]] \) time(s) from \( x_0=[[x0]] \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign inside the parentheses.
This forgets to multiply by \( x_0 \).
This uses 1 instead of 2 as the constant.
This jumps straight to the true reciprocal, ignoring the finite number of iterations.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
